summary: When parallel spec authoring produces 3+ cross-spec contract drifts, write a small shared-contracts.md that locks contested decisions (C1-Cn) plus a precedence rule. Each per-deliverable spec carries a one-line pointer at the top. Reviewers honor shared-contracts on any conflict.
detail: After dispatching 5 parallel spec authors, dual review surfaced 8 cross-spec drifts (event-type granularity, naming, CLI flags, stdout shape, exit codes, details placement, taxonomy walk syntax, Go file layout). Authoring shared-contracts.md (with C9 precedence rule) was faster than rewriting 5 specs. Implement step honored shared-contracts on every conflict; D2 reviewer noted 2 spec-vs-shared-contracts drifts that were correctly resolved per C9.

summary: Prefer per-hook event types (1:1 mapping) over collapsed semantic categories. Collapsed types push condition-branching into Go handlers and re-expand them via payload discriminators; per-hook types keep dispatch trivial.
detail: D2 spec proposed 7 collapsed event types (e.g., one pre_write covering 3 hooks); shared-contracts C1 locked 10 per-hook types. Implementation followed C1; result is a clean handler registry with no payload discriminator logic. Drift-guard test asserts bidirectional parity between YAML catalog and Go registry.

summary: For CLIs that may emit zero, one, or multiple results, always emit a JSON array (never a bare object). Empty array means no result. Branching shapes doubles caller code paths and breaks byte-equality parity tests.
detail: D2 spec allowed bare-object stdout for single-emit and array for multi-emit; shared-contracts C2 locked array-always. test-blocker-parity.sh uses jq -S over the array uniformly; no special-case handling needed for single vs multi.

summary: Cross-model reviewers can hallucinate test failures that don't reproduce locally, often due to sandbox-environment quirks (read-only /tmp, missing fixtures). Always verify a flagged correctness FAIL by running the test in your own environment before treating it as a real issue.
detail: D3 cross-model review claimed test-precommit-block.sh fails 8/14 with shims sourcing blocker_emit.sh from a non-existent fixture-derived root. Local re-run on the same branch showed 14/14 PASS. D4 cross-model also reported live-test failures caused by codex's read-only /tmp, not by the tests themselves.

summary: In dual-runtime architectures, place all domain rules (what counts as a blocker, what envelope is emitted) in the backend. Adapters translate host events to normalized backend input and translate backend output to host-native exit codes/UI. Adapters must not re-implement enforcement.
detail: core-adapter-boundary.md anchors this. D3 reduced 10 hooks averaging 43 executable lines to canonical 4-step shims averaging 5-13 lines (<=30 budget) by routing through furrow guard. 540 lines removed from bin/frw.d/hooks/. The same canonical envelope reaches both Claude (via shim exit codes) and Pi (via tool_call interceptor) without duplicated decision logic.

summary: For cross-runtime parity tests, the structural anti-cheat is two assertions: (1) every adapter shim invokes the canonical backend via subprocess (grep for the binary call); (2) no shim contains a literal envelope JSON. Without these, a shim can mimic backend output and pass parity silently.
detail: D4 test-blocker-parity.sh implements both: subprocess-invocation grep for furrow_guard|emit_canonical_blocker, and negative grep for hand-rolled code-literal JSON. The grep pair is mechanical to maintain and surfaces drift instantly.

summary: In phased migration, when a parity test compares output between adapters routing through the same backend, codes whose adapter handler doesn't yet exist would trivially pass parity (both sides invoke the same binary). Solution: skip those codes with an explicit reason naming the follow-up TODO. Re-activates as adapter handlers land.
detail: D4 test-blocker-parity.sh skips the 13 currently-shim-emitted codes with logged reason naming follow-up TODO pi-tool-call-canonical-schema-and-surface-audit. As Pi handlers wire in validate-actions.ts, those skips deactivate, growing parity coverage organically.
